source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 78 | score=6] A Depth Unit is a number of feet equal to the particular creature's maximum depth. (Thus the exact value of each creature's Depth Unit varies with the creature.) For example, the Depth Unit for a dwarf is 600 feet. If a dwarf fails his saving throw vs. paralyzation, he suffers 4410 plus an additional 1410 points of damage at depths of 601 to 1199 feet. At depths between 1200 and 1799 feet, a failed save would cause a dwarf 4410 plus an additional 2410 points of damage, as he would be 2 Depth Units beyond his maximum. A successful saving throw vs. paralyzation, however, indicates that the PC in question can function at an additional Depth Unit—albeit less efficiently—for up to one day, The cr
[PDF_PAGE 123 | score=3] Abyssal Hills and Plains Check for random encounters twice a day. An encounter occurs on a roll of 1 on 146. Table 51: Abyssal Hills and plains Encounters (412, d8) 2 Eye of the Deep 3 Dragon Turtle 4 Snake, giant sea 5 Shark, giant/Whale, giant 6 — Shark (7-8 HD) 7 Shark (5-6 HD) 8 Whale, killer 9 Octopus 10 Eel, marine 1l Aquatic race (roll on Table 57) 12 Hippocampus/Kapoacinth 13 Fish school or special 16 Dragonfish 17 Hazard (see page 125) 18 Giant 19 Squid, giant 20 Morkoth Oceanic Trench Because these areas are home to rather large and dangerous creatures, the trench encounter table contains only a few possible encounters. DMs should use this table cautiously, as one random encounter 
[PDF_PAGE 124 | score=3] Table 55: Coral Reef Encounters (412, 48) 2 Shark, giant 3 — Ray, ixitxachitl 4 Giant 5 Shark (7-8 HD) 6 Urchin, red 7 Sunken ship or special 8 — Shark (5-6 HD) 9 Fish school 10 Eel, weed 11 Shark (3-4 HD) 12 Aquatic race (roll on Table 57) 13 Urchin, green 14 Ray, sting, 15 Crab, giant 16 Lobster, giant (crayfish) 17 Hazard (see page 125) 18 Fel, marine 19 Worm, mottled 20 Urchin, silver/Urchin, yellow Rivers and Lakes Roll for random encounters twice a day. Encounters occur ona roll of 1 on 1d6. (The DM may wish to use the freshwater encounter tables from MONSTROUS COMPENDIUM" Annual, Volume Two instead of the one given here.) [PDF_PAGE 75 | score=2] water, the sounds of a person swimming, or the sounds made by a person running across the bottom deck of a ship. Heavy surface activity refers to things such as surface combat, dropping anchor, and construction, Underwater Activity refers to normal sounds produced by undersea creatures in their own environment—such as the normal clicks, whistles, and buzzes made by small sea creatures, Moderate underwater activity includes the use of tools underwater, the sounds produced by Medium to Huge marine creatures, and the sounds of combat with non- metallic weapons. Heavy underwater activity includes combat with surface weapons, spell effects, and underwater construction. Speaking Humans, demihumans
[PDF_PAGE 77 | score=2] Movement Surface dwellers suffer a distinct disadvantage when attempting to move underwater. Currents, pressure, and resistance severely limit a creature's ability to travel in a watery environment. Of course, magical items (such as a ring of free action) and magic spells (like teleport) can partially or completely negate such difficulties. However, characters without magical assistance must either find a solid surface upon which to walk, or swim to their destinations. Swimming Underwater Adventurers who decide to swim underwater face a difficult task, Water resistance and the additional weight of adventuring gear combine to slow the progress of a swimming character. Nonproficient swimmers (
[PDF_PAGE 105 | score=2] This spell does not allow the recipient to breathe underwater, nor does it grant him any extended aquatic movement ability. All the normal penalties for underwater combat and spellcasting (See Chapter 6) apply to the recipient. Furthermore, this dweomer has no effect on aquatic animals or creatures from the Elemental Plane of Water. The spell does not impair a creature's ability to drink water. It does not prevent powders and dusts from being dispersed in water, nor does it allow objects to burn underwater. New 3rd-Level Wizard Spell Pressure Resistance (Abjuration, Level 3) Range: Touch Duration: 1 turn + 1 turn/level Area of Effect: 1 Creature Components: 5, М Casting Time: 3 Saving Throw:
[PDF_PAGE 25 | score=1] Table 6: Disibility Ranges Condition Мут Spotted Type ID Detail Clearsky 2000 1500 500 100 10 Fog, dense 10 10 5 5 5 Fog light ^ 500 200 10 30 10 Fog, 10 50 25 15 10 moderate Mist, 1,000 500 250 30 10 light rain Night, 200 100 30 10 5 full moon Night, 10 50 do 5 3 moonless Twilight 500 300 150 30 10 Movement (Мот) indicates the maximum distance at which a moving figure or object can be seen. The observer notes only that something is moving and whether it rests on the surface or moves through the air. Ships under sail or oar power are considered moving objects. Spotted is the maximum distance a moving or stationary figure can be seen. Drifting objects are considered stationary for purposes of
[PDF_PAGE 28 | score=1] Uninhabited Coastline Uninhabited Coastlines refer to any area within 10 miles of a wild or untamed coastline—that is, any shore not settled by humans, intelligent humanoids, or demihumans. DMs should check for encounters twice daily. Encounters occur on a roll of 1 on a six-sided die. Cable 8: Uninbabited Coastline Encounters (48, 412) Riptide (see page 29) Shipwreck 2 3 4 Sahuagin/Seawolf, greater 5 Merman/Seawolf, lesser 6 Natural Phenomenon (roll on Table 10) 7 Shark (5-6 HD) 8 бапа Bar (see page 29) 9 Dolphin/Narwhal 10 Shallows (see page 29) 11 Sea Lion 12 Reef (see page 29) 13 Floating Seaweed (see page 29) 14 Giant 15 Pirate/Buccaneer 16 Sprite, sea 17 Rocky Shoals (see page 29) 18 S
